LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS POPE & POPE PROPERTIES, LLC AND JAMES D. POPE'S DEMURRER TO VERIFIED SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. WHETHER THE NOTE WAS AN INTEGRATED DOCUMENT IS NOT AN ISSUE THAT SHOULD BE DECIDED ON DEMURRER. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 2ND AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATION THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT INTEND TO REPAY THE LOAN WITHIN 6 MONTHS WOULD NOT HAVE ACCRUED UNTIL 10/4/07. THEREFORE THE FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION IS TIMELY ON THIS GROUND WHICH IS ENOUGH TO SAVE THE ENTIRE CAUSE OF ACTION ON DEMURRER. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER ON THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD THE TIME AND MANNER OF DISCOVERY AND THE INABILITY TO HAVE MADE EARLIER DISCOVERY DESPITE REASONABLE DILIGENCE. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO PLEAD AROUND THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS AT EH DEMURRER STAGE. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 7TH AND 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF LOUIS NEFF NOTICE OF HEARING TO DETERMINE VALIDITY OF KOCKELMAN THIRD PARTY CLAIM.

APPEARANCES: William Webb Farrer, Esq. of Law Offices of William Webb Farrer 765-9100 for pltf Louis Neff; Timothy M. Flaherty, Esq. of Morris Polich & Purdy LLP 984-8507 for Third-Party Claimants Peter Kockelman and Nicola Kockelman.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted - THE THIRD PARTY CLAIM FOR SUPERIOR SECURITY INTEREST IS GRANTED. A SECURITY INTEREST FIRST IN TIME RECORDED IS SUPERIOR. CCP SECTION 2897. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS 1, 2 AND 5 ARE OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS 3 AND 4 ARE SUSTAINED.
Plaintiff's further objection to the portion of the first sentence in paragraph 6 of the Nicola Kockelman declaration which states "In approximately November 2010 Pope entered into a real estate purchase agreement to sell the property" is heresay but is admitted under the state of mind exception to explain her actions as described in the rest of that paragraph and not for the truth of the matter stated.
Plaintiff withdrew his objection #3. The Court will therefore consider the statement in paragraph 8 and the evidence contained in exhibit 8.
Mr. Flaherty to prepare a proposed form of order. Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS POPE & POPE PROPERTIES, LLC AND JAMES D. POPE'S DEMURRER TO VERIFIED SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: William Webb Farrer, Esq. of Law Offices of William Webb Farrer 765-9100 for pltf & opposing party; no other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant's Demurrer to the 1st cause of action is overruled. Whether the Note was an integrated document is not an issue that should be decided on demurrer.
Defendant's Demurrer to the 2nd and 3rd causes of action is overruled. Plaintiff's allegation that Defendant did not intend to repay the loan within 6 months would not have accrued until 10/4/07. Therefore the fraud cause of action is timely on this ground which is enough to save the entire cause of action on demurrer.
Defendant's Demurrer on the 5th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead the time and manner of discovery and the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence.
Defendant's Demurrer to the 4th cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts to plead around the Statute of Limitations at eh demurrer stage.
Defendant's Demurrer to the 7th and 8th causes of action is overruled. Although the Court agrees that plaintiff's pursuit of these causes of action and onerous discovery despite Defendant's stipulation is on the verge of oppressive litigation and a waste of attorney and court resources (which the Court frowns upon), there is nothing that legally precludes his pursuing these claims. The Court is ordering the parties to a settlement conference within the next 30 days. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Joanne Farrell, CSR #4838

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Webb Farrer (765.9100) for plaintiff and responding party; Thomas Lalanne (434.1122) for defendants and moving parties

Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS POPE & POPE PROPERTIES, LLC, JAMES POPE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiff must demonstrate that the action is outside the restraints of C.C.P. section 726 by pleading that the deed of trust is a nullity and facts showing how/why the deed is a nullity. Plaintiff must plead facts showing the representations were false to support the fraud cause of action. Demurrer would be overruled as to usury which may be raised as an affirmative defense. Defendants to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
